critical_invariants: Serial port base address 0x3F8 (COM1) is hardware-defined and immutable
Serial port register offsets (THR=0, RBR=0, IER=1, FCR=2, LCR=3, MCR=4, LSR=5) are fixed
PS/2 controller ports (0x60 data, 0x64 status/command) are hardware-defined
PS/2 protocol: must check status register bit 0 before reading data port
VGA text mode memory at 0xB8000 with 2-byte character format (char + attribute)
Linear framebuffer (LFB) base address comes from VESA/GOP - must not be hardcoded
Serial LSR (Line Status Register) bit 5 must be checked before transmitting
known_opportunities: Output buffering: collect characters and flush in batch to reduce per-byte I/O port overhead
Serial FIFO: enable and use 16550 FIFO (FCR) for burst writes up to 16 bytes
REP OUTSB for bulk serial output instead of individual OUT instructions
Framebuffer scrolling: use REP MOVSQ for fast memory-to-memory scroll instead of byte copy
Double-buffering for LFB to reduce visible tearing
PS/2 keyboard scancode table: align to cache line for fast lookup
Combine VGA attribute and character into single 16-bit write
